mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market and broader economic contraction, which typically leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:historical recessions often follow unemployment spikes
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where the UNRATE trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also high, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment aimed at curbing inflation. This policy dampens economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is above 5%.

mechanism_narrative: In a confirmed period of strong economic growth (high GDPC1), a robust increase in housing starts (HOUST) acts as a leading indicator of future economic expansion and consumer confidence, driving positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: During periods of confirmed economic expansion (GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized), SPX 5-day returns do not show a positive response to HOUST increases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, indicating strong corporate profitability and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward returns of the S&P 500 exhibit a negative or zero correlation with the month-over-month growth of INDPRO over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in non-farm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth, falling below a critical threshold, indicates a weakening labor market and potential economic slowdown, leading to negative equity market sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:employment growth is key for consumer spending
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS growth falls below 100,000 over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is declining while retail sales (RSAFS) are simultaneously showing strong growth, it signals an accommodative monetary policy supporting robust consumer demand, which is highly bullish for equity markets over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of periods where FEDFUNDS is falling and RSAFS is growing strongly.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, unexpected decline in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a sudden drop in consumer spending, which can immediately trigger negative market sentiment and lead to short-term negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending drives short-term market reactions
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a month-over-month decline in RSAFS greater than 1.0%.
